messages.subject: [R-meta] 2-day online course on Introduction to Git and GitHub for Reproducible Research - 13–14 October
messages.subject_clean: [R-meta] 2-day online course on Introduction to Git and GitHub for Reproducible Research - 13–14 October
messages.body_plain:
Dear all,

Boost your research reproducibility and collaboration skills! Join our all-virtual 2-day course on Introduction to Git and GitHub for Reproducible Research, taking place 13?14 October.
 
Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/version-control/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/version-control/ )
 
In this course, you?ll learn how to:

Manage project versions with Git

Share and collaborate via GitHub

Apply best practices for reproducible research

Use branching, pull requests, and resolve merge conflicts
No prior experience with version control is needed!
 
Best regards,
Carlo
 
 
 
 
--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D


Physalia-courses DIRECTOR

info at physalia-courses.org

mobile: +49 17645230846

[ Bluesky ]( https://bsky.app/profile/physaliacourses.bsky.social ) [ Linkedin ]( https://www.linkedin.com/in/physalia-courses-a64418127/ )


	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.body_plain:
Dear Mario,

You are properly registered. It has simply been quiet around here lately. You can also check the archives:

https://stat.ethz.ch/pipermail/r-sig-meta-analysis/

Best,
Wolfgang

messages.subject: [R-meta]  2-day online course on Introduction to Git and GitHub for Reproducible Research - 13–14 October
messages.subject_clean: [R-meta]  2-day online course on Introduction to Git and GitHub for Reproducible Research - 13–14 October
messages.body_plain:
Dear Carlo,

It has been repeatedly pointed out on various mailing lists that course announcements unrelated to the list contents are perceived as spam and I agree. Please only post course announcements that actually relate to research synthesis / meta-analysis to this list. Thanks.

Best,
Wolfgang

________________________________
From: R-sig-meta-analysis <r-sig-meta-analysis-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of info--- via R-sig-meta-analysis <r-sig-meta-analysis at r-project.org>
Sent: Saturday, October 4, 2025 12:56
To: r-sig-meta-analysis at r-project.org <r-sig-meta-analysis at r-project.org>
Cc: info at physalia-courses.org <info at physalia-courses.org>
Subject: [R-meta] 2-day online course on Introduction to Git and GitHub for Reproducible Research - 13?14 October


Dear all,

Boost your research reproducibility and collaboration skills! Join our all-virtual 2-day course on Introduction to Git and GitHub for Reproducible Research, taking place 13?14 October.

Course website: [ https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/version-control/ ]( https://www.physalia-courses.org/courses-workshops/version-control/ )

In this course, you?ll learn how to:

Manage project versions with Git

Share and collaborate via GitHub

Apply best practices for reproducible research

Use branching, pull requests, and resolve merge conflicts
No prior experience with version control is needed!

Best regards,
Carlo




--------------------

Carlo Pecoraro, Ph.D
